name: Metatron Cube
description: The Metatron Cube is a complex geometric figure composed of 13 equal circles with lines extending from the center of each circle to the center of all other twelve circles. Proponents claim it represents the 'Fruit of Life' and originates from the Flower of Life pattern, containing every shape that exists in the universe, including Platonic solids. It is often associated with Archangel Metatron, who is believed to oversee the flow of energy in creation and provide a connection to the divine. The cube is considered a powerful symbol in sacred geometry and is used for healing, meditation, and spiritual transformation. Some believe it holds keys to understanding the universe's structure and the nature of reality. Claims often link it to the Kabbalah, alchemy, and other esoteric traditions. Mainstream science views the Metatron Cube and its interpretations as pseudoscience, lacking empirical evidence. Geometric patterns are recognized in nature, but assigning spiritual significance or attributing specific powers to them is not supported by scientific methodology. The connections drawn to religious and esoteric traditions are seen as interpretations based on subjective perspectives rather than demonstrable historical or scientific facts. While geometric art and symbolism can be meaningful and aesthetically pleasing, attributing supernatural powers or claiming it holds the key to universal understanding falls outside the realm of scientific inquiry.
subtitle: Sacred Geometry and Archangel Metatron

claims.claim: The Metatron Cube contains all Platonic solids.
claims.evidence: Visual analysis of the cube shows the presence of the five Platonic solids.
claims.criticism: While the Platonic solids can be visually extracted, this doesn't imbue the cube with inherent power or prove a divine origin. It's a geometric construction with specific properties.

claims.claim: It is a symbol of Archangel Metatron's energy and power.
claims.evidence: Spiritual practitioners report feeling a connection to Metatron when meditating on the cube.
claims.criticism: Personal experiences and subjective feelings are not verifiable scientific evidence. Such claims are based on belief systems and interpretations.

claims.claim: It can be used for healing and spiritual transformation.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal evidence from individuals who have used the cube in healing practices.
claims.criticism: Anecdotal evidence lacks scientific rigor and can be attributed to the placebo effect or confirmation bias. No controlled studies support healing claims.

keyFigures.name: Drunvalo Melchizedek
keyFigures.role: Promoter of Flower of Life and Metatron Cube concepts

keyFigures.name: Noam Sienna
keyFigures.role: Scholar of Jewish mysticism, providing context for Kabbalistic connections

timeline.event: Geometric patterns observed and used in art and architecture.
Popularization of the Flower of Life and Metatron Cube through New Age movements.
Continued use and discussion of the Metatron Cube in spiritual and esoteric circles.

sources.author: Melchizedek, Drunvalo
sources.work: The Ancient Secret of the Flower of Life, Vol. 1 & 2
sources.title: Melchizedek, Drunvalo

sources.author: Schneider, Michael S.
sources.work: A Beginner's Guide to Constructing the Universe: The Mathematical Archetypes of Nature, Art, and Science
sources.title: Schneider, Michael S.
